title: Make the locus of hover controls discoverable
statement: When a control appears on hover, show a quiet at-rest cue for its location and support keyboard focus plus a tooltip; users must not memorize invisible hit areas.
rationale: Progressive disclosure should reduce clutter without making the affordance impossible to locate.

prefer: Leave a quiet positional cue at rest.
Reveal the control on hover and focus with an accessible name and concise tooltip.
avoid: Hover-only access with no keyboard path.

when: A control is hidden until hover to reduce density.
not_when: The control is always visible or the entire containing object is the target.

evidence.summary: Requested a persistent drag-handle cue and accurate tooltip so its location is not guesswork before hover.
Required comment-list interaction to open the comment at its actual gutter anchor, not from a detached list control.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Leave a quiet positional cue at rest.
Reveal the control on hover and focus with an accessible name and concise tooltip.
negative_examples: Hover-only access with no keyboard path.

checks: Can a user locate the affordance before hovering precisely?
Does keyboard focus reveal and operate it?
Is the tooltip supplemental rather than essential?
